**Marian Bayoff Ilitch** (born January 7, 1933) is an American billionaire businesswoman, and the co-founder of Little Caesars Pizza with her late husband, Mike Ilitch . As of March 2018, Ilitch was one of the world's seven richest women, according to Bloomberg . <sup>[[ 1 ]]()</sup>

title: Little Caesars®|Hometown Pride
excerpts:
Section Title: Sports
Content:
Today, the Ilitches are part of two of the most exciting teams in professional sports: the Detroit Red Wings (purchased by Mike and Marian in 1982) and the Detroit Tigers (purchased by Mike in 1992). Under Ilitch ownership, the Red Wings have won 16 divisional championships, six President’s Trophies (for the season-best record among all NHL teams), six Campbell Bowls and four Stanley Cups. The team has played post-season hockey for 25 consecutive years — the most in all of current professional sports. In 2014, ground was broken on a new home for the Red Wings, now called Little Caesars Arena, which forms the heart of The District Detroit. This project, led by Olympia Development, is designed to renovate 50 blocks of Detroit into a walkable, five-neighborhood community with residential, sports and entertainment venues.

title: Entrepreneur and Philanthropist Marian Ilitch’s Lifetime Achievements and Contributions Celebrated by Thousands Whose Lives She’s Touched
excerpts:
During the event, Michigan Governor Gretchen Whitmer declared May 8, 2024 ‘Marian Ilitch Day,’ explaining that the date represents the day in 1959 when Marian and Mike co-founded a small business, Little Caesars Pizza Treat. Through years of hard work, Little Caesars is now the third-largest pizza chain in the world.“
To honor Marian, I encourage every Michigander to shop at a small business on Marian Ilitch Day. There is no better way to honor the impact of Mrs. I than by uplifting the small business owners who are the backbone of our state’s economy,” said Whitmer. “Today, and every day, let’s work hard to follow in Marian’s footsteps and build a Michigan where everyone can succeed.”
Always entrepreneurs at heart, Marian and Mike expanded their interests over the years to include food, sports, entertainment, gaming and other industries.

title: Little Caesars - Wikipedia
excerpts:
Section Title: History
Content:
[edit ]
Little Caesars Pizza was founded on May 8, 1959, by the married couple Mike Ilitch and Marian Ilitch . The first location was in a strip mall in Garden City, Michigan , a suburb of Detroit, and named "Little Caesar's Pizza Treat". The original store closed in October 2018, relocating down the street to a new building in nearby Westland . <sup>[[ 4 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 5 ]]()</sup>
The first Little Caesar's franchise location opened in 1962 in Warren, Michigan , and was still called Little Caesar's Pizza Treat. The same year the Little Caesar's logo became a 3D figure and was used in outdoor signage. <sup>[[ 6 ]]()</sup>
Caesar's design used from 1988 to 2017

title: Little Caesars®|Our History
excerpts:
Section Title: Our Story > Our Founders
Content:
The global chain that Little Caesars is today began with a blind date between Mike Ilitch and Marian Bayoff that was arranged by Mike’s father in 1954. Within just a matter of months, the couple was married.
Section Title: Our Story > 1959
Content:
Mike and Marian Ilitch invest their life savings into their first pizza restaurant in Garden City, Michigan, a suburb of Detroit. Marian carefully recorded Little Caesars very first sales in a spiral notebook.
 ... 
Section Title: Our Story > 1969
Content:
Little Caesars celebrates the opening of its 50th restaurant. It also went international with the opening of its first restaurant in Canada. Today, the company has restaurants in all 50 states and 18 international markets.
Mike and Marian have always considered Little Caesars employees as one big family. "I consider Little Caesars my eighth child," said Marian.

title: Marian Ilitch’s Lifetime Support of Family, Colleagues, and Community Laid the Foundation for Ilitch Companies’ Continued Growth and Positive Impact
excerpts:
Before marrying her husband and lifetime business partner, Mike Ilitch, Marian worked as a reservation clerk for what would become Delta Airlines in Detroit, where was captivated by the hustle and bustle of working downtown during Detroit’s prime. While Marian was clear with her parents that she did not wish to date a Macedonian boy, she was intrigued by the possibility of going out with a baseball player – Mike Ilitch was both. Not long after meeting, she and Mike were married. They eventually would become parents to seven children, 21 grandchildren and 21 great-grandchildren, and always made family time a priority. An Entrepreneurial Spirit
After Marian and Mike’s third child was born, the couple invested their savings to open their own business – a single pizza shop in Garden City, Michigan called Little Caesars Pizza Treat. A few years later, they explored an exciting new business model called franchising and in 1962, the first Little Caesars franchise was sold in Warren, Michigan.

title: Ilitch Holdings
excerpts:
Section Title: Background
Content:
[edit ]
Mike and Marian Ilitch started Little Caesars in 1959. A complementary business, Blue Line Foodservice, was soon started to supply ingredients and other products to the growing restaurant chain. <sup>[[ 4 ]]()</sup>
In 1982, Ilitch entered into sports with the purchase of the struggling Red Wings. Ilitch, executing his long-term Detroit business conglomerate plan, also purchased Olympia Stadium Corporation that year. <sup>[[ 4 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 5 ]]()</sup> Olympia Stadium operated the Joe Louis and Cobo arenas . <sup>[[ 5 ]]()</sup>
The couple purchased and restored the downtown Fox Theatre in 1987. <sup>[[ 4 ]]()</sup> In 1988, they fielded an indoor football team, Detroit Drive , in the Arena Football League . <sup>[[ 6 ]]()</sup> In 1992, Mike purchased the Detroit Tigers . <sup>[[ 4 ]]()</sup> Purchasing the Tigers led him to sell the Drive in February 1994 given the leagues' overlapping schedules. <sup>[[ 6 ]]()</sup>
